sanskrit:
अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून् वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः।
निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम्॥
transliteration:
avācya-vādāṃś ca bahūn vadiṣyanti tavāhitāḥ |
nindantas tava sāmarthyaṃ tato duḥkhataraṃ nu kim ||
literal_meaning: Unspeakable/abusive words and many | will speak | your | enemies | while vilifying | your | capability | then | more painful | indeed | what
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns Arjuna that if he retreats from battle his detractors will publicly shame him, questioning his competence and courage—social humiliation being a deeper wound than death.
psychological_state: Anticipatory shame spiral: the ego forecasts reputational ruin, triggering a freeze-fight response rooted in tribal survival needs.
trigger_scenario: A professional considering whistle-blowing fears becoming the office pariah—‘What will they say about me at the Christmas party?’
anchor_text: Stand firm; retreat invites harsher pain than death.
